attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Splash Burn
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Iron Crusher
attacks.effect: You may discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon or a Stadium card in play.

attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stormy Seas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Splash Burn
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

This card can only be attached to Water Pokémon. This card provides Water Energy only while this card is attached to a Water Pokémon.

If the Water Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, put that Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to it.)

(If this card is attached to anything other than a Water Pokémon, discard this card.)

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Scizor-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Choose which way this card faces before you play it. Any damage done to this ↓ player's Metal Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Choose which way this card faces before you play it. The attacks of this ↓ player's Darkness Pokémon do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

You may play 2 Puzzle of Time cards at once.
• If you played 1 card, look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.
• If you played 2 cards, put 2 cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

Discard as many cards as you like from your hand. Then, draw that many cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Discard a card from your hand. If you do, look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, discard all Energy attached to that Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach a basic Energy card you find there to a Basic Pokémon on your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gyarados-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Basic Pokémon this card is attached to gets +40 HP and its attacks do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, your opponent discards 3 cards from his or her hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, your opponent discards 3 cards from his or her hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, your opponent discards 3 cards from his or her hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your opponent's turn.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Once during each player's turn, if that player's Active Pokémon is Asleep, he or she may remove that Special Condition and heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Historically, in the Kalos region, these Pokémon were the designated guardians of the king.

attacks.name: Nyan Press
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

attacks.name: Act Cute
attacks.effect: Your opponent puts a card from his or her hand on the bottom of his or her deck.

flavorText: When it's happy, Glameow demonstrates beautiful movements of its tail, like a dancing ribbon.

attacks.name: Elemental Feather
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon).

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mystifying Horns
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Big Charge
attacks.effect: If you have any Mega Evolution Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It digs into the ground with its tail and makes a mazelike nest. It can fly just a little.

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.effect: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Raticate BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Dirty Shock
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to that Pokémon.

flavorText: The webs on its hind legs enable it to cross rivers. It searches wide areas for food.

attacks.name: Dangerous Suspicion
attacks.effect: Draw a card. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its fangs are long and very sharp. They grow continuously, so it gnaws on hard things to whittle them down.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your deck.

flavorText: Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.

attacks.name: Dizzying Cologne
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its scent is so overpowering that, unless a Trainer happens to really enjoy the smell, he or she will have a hard time walking alongside it.
In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked.

attacks.name: Mighty Wind
attacks.effect: You may attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hurricane Wing
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Moon Barrier
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Dragon Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

attacks.name: Spike Lash
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon for each Colorless in that Pokémon's Retreat Cost. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They attach themselves to cave ceilings, firing steel spikes at targets passing beneath them.

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: It absorbs the iron it finds in the rock while clinging to the ceiling. It shoots spikes when in danger.

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

attacks.name: Iron Crusher
attacks.effect: You may discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon or a Stadium card in play.

rules:
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Parting Shot
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 60 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Buster Swing
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It charges ahead and bashes its opponents like a berserker, uncaring about any hits it might take. Its arms are mighty enough to snap a telephone pole.

attacks.name: Dark Pulse
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Darkness Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Head
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 80 more damage.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Roll Up
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. If any of them are heads, your opponent reveals his or her hand. Then, for each heads, discard a card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Otherworldly Return
attacks.effect: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: By flapping its leafy fan, it can whip up gusts of 100 feet per second that can level houses.

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.
It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can't help grinning if anyone pats its head.

attacks.name: Turbo Assault
attacks.effect: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bite Off
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It flies at speeds equals to a jet fighter plane. It never allows its prey to escape.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: As it digs to expand its nest, it habitually digs up gems that it then hoards in its nest.

attacks.name: Never Enough
attacks.effect: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It nests in small, horizontal holes in cave walls. It pounces to catch prey that stray too close.

attacks.name: Watch and Learn
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Pokémon used an attack during his or her last turn, use it as this attack.

flavorText: It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.

attacks.name: Silent Fear
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Trevenant BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It's kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: According to old tales, these Pokémon are stumps possessed by the spirits of children who died while lost in the forest.

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution.

attacks.name: Painful Sword
attacks.effect: Double the number of damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Megaton Slash
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Apparently, it can detect innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.

attacks.name: Stab Deeply
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even for an opponent greatly accomplished at swordplay.

attacks.name: Stab
attacks.effect: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person's arm and drains that person's life energy completely.

attacks.name: Energy Present
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The eyeball patterns on the interior of its ears emit psychic energy. It keeps the patterns tightly covered because that power is too immense.
It has enough psychic energy to blast everything within 300 feet of itself, but it has no control over its power.

attacks.name: Offensive Bomb
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

attacks.name: Reflective Shield
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 5 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psy Report
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: The guardians of an ancient city, they always fly the same route while keeping watch for invaders.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.

attacks.name: Miraculous Shine
attacks.effect: Devolve each of your opponent's evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Seeing its swinging pendulum can induce sleep in three seconds, even in someone who just woke up.

attacks.name: Focused Wish
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: If you think that you had a good dream but you can't remember it, a Drowzee has probably eaten it.

attacks.name: Crashing Bolt
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has Fighting Resistance, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

attacks.name: Wild Fury
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Luxray BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Snarl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.
It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.
It loves to feed on strong electricity. It occasionally appears around large power plants and so on.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Greninja BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Shadow Stitching
attacks.effect: Until the end of your opponent's next turn, each Pokémon your opponent has in play, in his or her hand, and in his or her discard pile has no Abilities. (This includes cards that come into play on that turn.)

attacks.name: Moonlight Slash
attacks.effect: You may return a Water Energy from this Pokémon to your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It appears and vanishes with a ninja's grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water.

attacks.name: Water Duplicates
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Frogadier and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute's time.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

attacks.name: Siphon Off
attacks.effect: Attach 3 Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shaky Fall
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. That Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

attacks.name: Frog Hop
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to immobilize its opponents.

attacks.name: Round
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage times the number of your Pokémon in play that have the Round attack.

flavorText: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk.

attacks.name: Mineral Pump
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Turbo
attacks.effect: Search your deck for 2 Water Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Pearl Hurricane
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They prefer unpolluted southern seas. Their coral branches lose their color and deteriorate in dirty water.

attacks.name: Errand-Running
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Water Splash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its high intelligence enables it to understand human speech. It likes to ferry people on its back.

attacks.name: Blast Geyser
attacks.effect: You may do 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

rules:
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stormy Seas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Splash Burn
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

attacks.name: Sudden Grip
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved from Shellder during this turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.

attacks.name: Clamp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Clamping on to an opponent reveals its vulnerable parts, so it uses this move only as a last resort.

attacks.name: Razor Shell
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Clamping on to an opponent reveals its vulnerable parts, so it uses this move only as a last resort.

attacks.name: Psych Up
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Psych Up attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When its head was bitten, toxins entered Slowpoke's head and unlocked an extraordinary power.

attacks.name: Careless Head
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Walk-Off Homer
attacks.effect: If you use this attack when you have only 1 Prize card left, you win this game.

flavorText: When a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.

attacks.name: Tail Lure
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won't even notice for a whole day.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Golduck BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Derail
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The forelegs are webbed, helping to make it an adept swimmer. It can be seen swimming elegantly in lakes, etc.
It has mystical powers but doesn't recall that it has used them. That is why it always looks puzzled.

attacks.name: Reinforced Flame
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It draws in air through its tail, transforms it into fire, and uses it like a tongue. It melts Durant and eats them.

attacks.name: Spiral Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Strong Flare
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Eruption
attacks.effect: Each player discards the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

flavorText: If angered, the humps on its back erupt in a shower of molten lava. It lives in the craters of volcanoes.
The magma in its body reaches 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit. Its hump gets smaller when it uses Fire-type moves.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its magnificent bark conveys a sense of majesty. Anyone hearing it can't help but grovel before it.
It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

attacks.name: Mountain Munch
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Scrape Down
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, discard the top 4 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

attacks.name: Boo-Hoo
attacks.effect: If your opponent attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn, that Pokémon will be Asleep.

attacks.name: Leaf Slice
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Since they prefer moist, nutrient-rich soil, the areas where Petilil live are known to be good for growing plants.

attacks.name: Screech
attacks.effect: During your next turn, any damage done to the Defending Pokémon by attacks is increased by 60 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It signals its emotions with its melodies. Scientists are studying these melodic patterns.

attacks.name: Bug Hunch
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Grass Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.
When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

attacks.name: Green Force
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Meganium's breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The buds that ring its neck give off a spicy aroma that perks people up.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.
